recent_developments.headline: Premium valuation: ~30x forward earnings on a $4.3T base leaves the return dominated by multiple persistence
recent_developments.so_what: At ~30x forward P/E and ~27x EV/EBITDA, the market prices in durable premium growth on the largest company in the world. The law of large numbers makes sustaining mid-teens growth on a $451B revenue base progressively harder, so a reversion toward a market multiple would drive a large drawdown independent of business quality. Multiple compression — not the business — is the dominant, decision-relevant risk, and warrants a dedicated valuation review before sizing.

recent_developments.headline: Per-share growth is flattered by very large buybacks
recent_developments.so_what: Apple repurchases roughly $80-90B of stock annually, so reported EPS growth runs ahead of underlying net-income growth. The premium multiple should be judged against organic growth, not buyback-boosted EPS, or the entry looks cheaper than it is.

recent_developments.headline: Value concentrated in iPhone; Services is the key growth swing
recent_developments.so_what: iPhone still drives the majority of revenue and gross profit, and the premium multiple leans on Services continuing to grow faster than hardware. A hardware stumble or a Services deceleration would pressure both growth and the multiple at the same time.

recent_developments.headline: Elite cash generation is real but already in the price
recent_developments.so_what: ~$101B annual free cash flow and ~48% gross margins anchor the quality case, but at ~30x earnings that quality is largely reflected — it provides little valuation cushion if growth normalizes.

live_headlines.headline: SpaceX Market Cap Breaches $2.5 Trillion: Goes Past Tesla, TSMC And Broadcom
live_headlines.so_what: SpaceX stock jumped close to 20% on Monday, hitting a market cap of $2.52 trillion, outshining TSMC.

live_headlines.headline: With Roku Stock Near Highs, Is It Worth It?
live_headlines.so_what: The streaming giant's high-margin advertising business is firing on all cylinders, but you have to weigh that against a money-losing device segment and a premium price tag.
